title: NBA Daily News Digest
subtitle: March 26, 2026: Expansion Talks, Playoff Picture Solidifies, and Star Performances

items.headline: NBA Board of Governors Approves Exploration of Las Vegas and Seattle Expansion
items.summary: The NBA Board of Governors has officially voted to authorize the league to explore potential expansion teams in Las Vegas and Seattle, a significant step towards bringing the league to 32 clubs. New teams could potentially join by the 2028-29 season.

items.headline: Pistons and Thunder Clinch Top Conference Seeds
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons and Oklahoma City Thunder have officially secured the top seeds in the Eastern and Western Conferences, respectively, as the regular season winds down. The Pistons hold a 52-20 record, while the Thunder lead the West at 57-16.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Sidelined with Knee Injury
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo is currently sidelined with a left knee injury, impacting the Bucks' late-season push.

items.headline: Wizards Defeat Jazz in High-Scoring Affair
items.summary: The Washington Wizards routed the Utah Jazz with a final score of 133-110.

items.headline: Clippers Cruise Past Raptors
items.summary: Kawhi Leonard led the Los Angeles Clippers to a dominant 119-94 victory over the Toronto Raptors.

items.headline: Doncic's 43-Point Performance Lifts Lakers Over Pacers
items.summary: Luka Doncic exploded for 43 points as the Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Indiana Pacers 137-130.

items.headline: Jokic Triple-Double Powers Nuggets Past Suns
items.summary: Nikola Jokic recorded a triple-double to lead the Denver Nuggets to a narrow 125-123 win against the Phoenix Suns.

items.headline: Brunson Shines as Knicks Extend Win Streak Against Pelicans
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 32 points helped the New York Knicks secure a 121-116 victory over the New Orleans Pelicans, extending their winning streak.

items.headline: Mitchell's 42 Points Lead Cavaliers Over Magic
items.summary: Donovan Mitchell erupted for 42 points as the Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Orlando Magic 136-131.

items.headline: Trail Blazers Rout Bucks; Avdija Impresses
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers routed the Milwaukee Bucks 130-99. Scoot Henderson had 23 points, and Deni Avdija contributed 18 points and 5 rebounds for Portland.

items.headline: Spurs Dominate Grizzlies on Wembanyama's Big Night
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs easily handled the Memphis Grizzlies with a 123-98 win, highlighted by Victor Wembanyama's historic defensive performance.

items.headline: Hawks Edge Pistons in Overtime Thriller
items.summary: The Atlanta Hawks narrowly defeated the Detroit Pistons 130-129 in an overtime thriller.

items.headline: Timberwolves Stun Rockets with Record Overtime Comeback
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves achieved the NBA's largest overtime comeback on record, overcoming a 13-point deficit to stun the Houston Rockets.

items.headline: Charlotte Hornets vs. New York Knicks
Orlando Magic vs. Sacramento Kings
Detroit Pistons vs. New Orleans Pelicans
Philadelphia 76ers vs. Chicago Bulls
Boston Celtics vs. Oklahoma City Thunder
Cleveland Cavaliers vs. Miami Heat
Denver Nuggets vs. Dallas Mavericks
Golden State Warriors vs. Brooklyn Nets
Luka Doncic's Scoring Spree
items.summary: LAL's Luka Doncic lit up the scoreboard with 43 points against the Indiana Pacers.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell Dominates
items.summary: CLE's Donovan Mitchell poured in 42 points to lead his team over the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic Triple-Double Masterclass
items.summary: DEN's Nikola Jokic delivered a stellar triple-double with 23 points, 17 rebounds, and 17 assists against the Phoenix Suns.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama's Historic Defensive Display
items.summary: SAS rookie Victor Wembanyama showcased a historic defensive performance against the Memphis Grizzlies, being noted as the 9th player in the steals-and-blocks era to reach exceptional numbers in a single game with limited minutes.

items.headline: Ryan Rollins Career-High
items.summary: MIL's Ryan Rollins dropped a career-high 36 points against the Portland Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Continues Strong Play for Blazers
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija contributed 18 points, 5 rebounds, and 2 assists in the Portland Trail Blazers' dominant win over the Milwaukee Bucks. He is averaging 21.6 points over his last 10 games.

items.headline: Donovan Clingan Double-Double
items.summary: Rookie Donovan Clingan of the Trail Blazers posted a double-double with 14 points and 15 rebounds against the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Ja Morant Out for Season
items.summary: Memphis Grizzlies star Ja Morant will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season after suffering a UCL sprain in his left elbow.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham Sidelined with Lung Injury
items.summary: Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham is out due to a left lung pneumothorax and is expected to miss his fifth straight game tonight.

items.headline: Tyrese Maxey Out with Finger Strain
items.summary: Philadelphia 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey is out with a right finger tendon strain.

items.headline: Warriors Hit with Injuries: Curry Out, Moody Done for Season
items.summary: Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry will miss the next two games with a knee injury, while Moses Moody is out for the season with a torn patellar tendon.

items.headline: Lillard and Lively II Out for Season
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers' Damian Lillard is out for the season with an Achilles injury, and Dallas Mavericks' Dereck Lively II is also out for the season with a foot injury.
